panels.title: Read vs Write Ops/s
panels.description: Read and write throughput on the same chart to correlate degradation patterns.

panels.title: Read/Write Ratio
panels.description: Ratio of read to write operations. Useful to see if reads are being starved relative to writes.

panels.title: Query Timeout Rate
panels.description: Rate of CQL query timeouts over time. If this rises, the database is under pressure.

panels.title: Validation Row Results/s
panels.description: Breakdown of validation comparison results. 'missing in test' = data bug in Scylla under test. 'different values' = data corruption.

panels.title: Avg Rows Per Query
panels.description: Average number of rows returned per SELECT query. If this diverges between test and oracle, one side is losing data.

panels.title: Rows Returned/s
panels.description: Rate of rows returned from read queries, per cluster.

panels.title: Rows Per Query P50/P95/P99
panels.description: Distribution of rows returned per query. Shows whether some queries hit very large or empty partitions.

panels.title: Zero-Row Query Rate
panels.description: Rate of queries returning zero rows vs total queries. If zero-row rate grows, partitions are being deleted faster than data is being found.

panels.title: Zero-Row Query %
panels.description: Percentage of read queries that return zero rows. Rising % means gemini is reading empty/deleted partitions.

panels.title: Test vs Oracle Row Delta
panels.description: Difference in rows returned between test and oracle clusters. Should stay near 0. Divergence indicates data inconsistency.

panels.title: Deduplication & Errors
panels.description: Row deduplication rate indicates eventual consistency duplicates. Rising discrepancies = Scylla data bug.

panels.title: Context Cancellations/s
panels.description: Operations cancelled or timed out. Rising = database under pressure or network issues.

panels.title: Unique Prepared Statements
panels.description: Number of unique query strings seen. If this keeps growing, the query generator produces too many variants.

panels.title: New Statement Rate
panels.description: Rate of never-before-seen query strings. Should converge to 0 once all query shapes have been seen.

panels.title: Driver Retry Attempt Distribution
panels.description: Queries/s broken down by how many driver retries they needed. Green = succeeded first try, yellow/orange/red = needed retries.

panels.title: Driver Retry Rate
panels.description: Rate of queries that succeeded on first attempt vs required driver retries.

panels.title: Driver Errors by Type
panels.description: Driver errors classified by type: read_timeout, write_timeout, unavailable, overloaded, connection_closed, etc. Each type points to a different root cause.

panels.title: Driver Rows Per Page
panels.description: Rows observed per driver page. Different from gemini-level rows — this is what the driver sees per network round-trip.

panels.title: Query Latency by Attempt
panels.description: Query latency broken down by attempt number. If retries (attempt>0) have much higher latency, the retried queries hit slower nodes or overloaded paths.

panels.title: Driver Retry Percentage
panels.description: Percentage of queries that required at least one driver-level retry. Above 5% indicates systematic pressure.

panels.title: Configured Mutation Ratios
panels.description: Configured insert/update/delete ratios as set at startup.

panels.title: Actual Mutation Ratios
panels.description: Actual mutation ratios computed from executed statements. Insert includes both regular and JSON inserts. Update shows counter_update only (regular updates fall through to insert).

panels.title: Configured Select Ratios
panels.description: Configured select subtype ratios as set at startup.

panels.title: Actual Select Ratios
panels.description: Actual select subtype ratios from observed rates. Only available during read/mixed mode. Compare with configured to verify.

panels.title: Mutation Statements/s
panels.description: Rate of mutation statements generated by subtype (insert, insert_json, delete, counter_update).

panels.title: Select Statements/s
panels.description: Rate of select statements generated by subtype (single_partition, multi_partition, index).

panels.title: Configured Delete Subtypes
panels.description: Configured delete subtype ratios. Note: all subtypes currently fall through to deleteSinglePartition.

panels.title: Mutation Ratio Drift Over Time
panels.description: Intended ratios (ratio controller output) vs actual ratios (what was executed). The gap between intended update% and 0% actual update shows the update→insert fallback.

panels.title: Select Ratio Drift Over Time
panels.description: Select subtype ratios over time. Drift from configured indicates fallback paths being hit.

panels.title: Invalid Partitions
panels.description: Current number of permanently invalid partitions. Rising = validation failures accumulating.

panels.title: Partition Replacements/s
panels.description: Rate of partition slot replacements (deletes) and extensions (new slots).

panels.title: Deleted Partitions Pending
panels.description: Deleted partitions waiting for validation vs emission rate. Backlog = channel full or slow validation.

panels.title: Mutation Retries/s
panels.description: Mutation retry outcomes. 'exhausted' = mutations permanently failing.

panels.title: Validation Retry Queue
panels.description: Validation retry queue depth and flow. Rising 'pending' = retries accumulating faster than they complete.

panels.title: Worker Stop Events
panels.description: Workers stopped due to error budget exhaustion. If rising, errors are accumulating.

panels.title: Go Mutex Wait Time
panels.description: Time goroutines spend waiting on mutexes. Rising indicates lock contention.
shows how many heap objects are allocated. This is a counter value so you can use rate() to objects allocated/s.
panels.title: Rate of Objects Allocated
